expectations.text: Output contains an explicit gate verdict — either PASSED or BLOCKED
expectations.evidence: The report contains the verdict '✅ PASSED — Ready to open a PR.' twice: once in Step 6 Gate Decision (line 166) and once at the end of the report (line 186). The verdict is unambiguous and uses the exact PASSED keyword.

expectations.text: Output classifies findings into severity categories (critical, warning, or info)
expectations.evidence: Step 5 Aggregated Findings uses three explicit sections: '### Critical Issues' (None), '### Warnings' (8 items listed), and '### Info' (None). The Step 6 gate summary also uses '## Warnings (8)' and '## Info' headers with a structured table.

expectations.text: Output explicitly groups changed files as frontend (client/) vs backend (server/)
expectations.evidence: Step 1 'Changed Files Summary' uses explicit '### Frontend (client/)' and '### Backend (server/src/)' subsections. Backend section states 'No server/src/ files changed. Backend architecture review skipped.'

expectations.text: Output identifies missing test files for new or substantially modified components
expectations.evidence: Step 4 'Test coverage' section contains a table listing 7 source files without test files: AgentEditorPage.tsx, AgentCard.tsx, PRDetailView.tsx, ReviewRunAccordion.tsx, PullsListView.tsx, AppShell.tsx, and useShellContext.ts. Each is noted with a reason why coverage matters (e.g., 'conditional rendering and mutation logic').

expectations.text: The gate verdict is PASSED (no critical issues expected on this branch)
expectations.evidence: The gate verdict is '✅ PASSED — Ready to open a PR.' and '### Critical Issues: None.' is explicitly stated in Step 5. All 8 findings are classified as Warnings, not Critical.

claims.claim: 61 files changed, 12,138 insertions, 695 deletions in the full diff
claims.evidence: This matches expected output for the lab-2 branch which includes session logs, docs, and skill files in addition to product code. The report explicitly distinguishes client/src/ (1,264 lines) from total (12,833 lines).
The report explicitly states 'No server/src/ files changed. Backend architecture review skipped.' and lists only client/ and documentation files in the changed files summary.

claims.claim: PRDetailView.tsx is 185 lines — approaching the 200-line limit
claims.evidence: The file is listed as NEW and described consistently at 185 lines in both Step 2 (Anti-pattern check) and Step 5 Warnings. The line count is plausible given the described content.

claims.claim: Three new test files were added: ConfirmModal.test.tsx, FilterBar.test.tsx, PRRow.test.tsx
claims.evidence: All three are listed in both the Step 1 changed files summary (with 'NEW (test)' annotation) and the Step 4 test coverage section with checkmarks.

claims.claim: No critical frontend architecture violations found
claims.evidence: The report checks 7 anti-patterns (direct fetch in component body, business logic in page.tsx, misplaced 'use client', broken prop types, cross-feature _components imports, 200-line component body, useState for server state, useEffect dependency issues) and marks all clean except the PRDetailView near-limit warning.

suggestions.assertion: Output identifies missing test files for new or substantially modified components
suggestions.reason: This assertion passes even if the report only mentions one missing test. Consider strengthening it to require identification of ALL substantially modified components without tests — the current output found 7, but a minimal compliant output mentioning just one would also pass.

No assertion checks whether the anti-pattern scan was actually run (e.g., that 'use client' placement, fetch-in-component, and useState-for-server-state were explicitly verified). The report does include these checks, but an assertion would catch a future run that skips the architecture review entirely and only lists files.

No assertion verifies that the security review section is present and covers at least the core checks (hardcoded secrets, XSS, unvalidated input). The current run includes it, but a lazy execution could omit it and still pass all 5 expectations.

eval_feedback.overall: All 5 expectations are met with genuine substance. The main gap is that several important review sections (architecture anti-patterns, security review) have no corresponding assertion, so a future regression that omits those sections would still pass the eval.
